entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Zelensky visits troops as Putin issues threat to West

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: Putin warns of new targets; Zelenskiy visits troops on eastern front – live
  • Graham Brady reportedly set to announce vote of no confidence in Boris Johnson by Tory MPs – live
  • Beetle v killer cactus: Kenyan herders fight to stop a plant destroying their way of life
  • Island Records founder Chris Blackwell: ‘I’m interested in what’s different’
  • Home Office offers asylum seekers choice between war zones they fled and Rwanda
  • Capitol attack panel to unveil new evidence against Trump at public hearings
  • ‘Lost’ alligator found in west Texas desert in ‘rare sighting’
  • ‘Tomorrow, tomorrow, Putin will come’: the 2014 Ukraine refugees forced to flee for a second time
  • ‘Nine Inch Nails is inappropriate for a Christmas party’: St Vincent’s honest playlist
  • Some people think they would rather die than have help brushing their teeth – but care is not tragic | Lucy Webster
  • ‘Bloody cheek, I’m not ageing’: Simon Hattenstone interviews his 94-year-old mum, Marje
  • A new start after 60: ‘There she was – my actual mother. “You took your time!” she said’
  • US bird flu outbreak: millions of birds culled in ‘most inhumane way available’

  • ‘Our country is getting old’: the man changing how Brazil sees dementia

  • Friendlyjordies: John Barilaro to be paid $715,000 by Google over YouTube videos

  • ‘Hidden world’ of marine life discovered in Antarctic ‘river’ under ice
  • Warriors level NBA finals as Poole’s halfcourt buzzer-beater polishes win over Celtics

  • ‘At least be bearable’: in the big business of music for kids, parents are the final frontier
  • After winning the climate election, Labor must be the climate government the whole world needs | Bill Hare
  • The Guardian view on the Tory leadership: time to put up or shut up | Editorial
  • From a turkey slap to a chocolate bath: the 10 most memorable Australian reality TV moments
  • Platinum jubilee celebrations through Guardian lenses
  • Nigeria: gunmen kill dozens in ‘satanic’ attack on Catholic church
  • Joe Root wins Test for England having been left to do what he does best | Andy Bull
  • Tory MPs spend jubilee weekend placating angry voters
  • Diabetes drug leads to notable weight loss in people with obesity – study
  • Rafael Nadal defies time and Casper Ruud to win his 14th French Open title
  • Thousands of Britons stuck at airports as easyJet cancels another 80 flights
  • ‘I believe in our army’: life in Donbas as the frontline creeps closer
  • Why does tinned food come in a 400g can?
  • Teenager Leila Mottley on writing her debut novel: ‘I’ve always done things at a warp speed’
  • Readers reply: what is the most dangerous food in the world?
  • Cheerio, Duckie: regulars look back at the LGBTQ+ club that broke the mould
  • Nigel Slater’s goat’s cheese tart and gooseberry pudding recipes
  • Another fine mess: clearing up the dog poo problem
  • Surf schools: five of the best places in the UK to learn
  • The great Coronapause is over, but history tells us that complacency can be a killer | Mark Honigsbaum
  • Ex-nurse Christie Watson: ‘Women’s health has always been undervalued’

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-06 06:53AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Man slashed in knife attack in New Territories village, Hong Kong police search for suspects
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts warn of rebound in cases, elderly urged to get Covid shots as soon as possible
  • LME faces Elliott International’s US$456 million lawsuit for suspending nickel futures trading, cancelling trades
  • Wear masks over long term to reduce antibiotics use, University of Hong Kong researchers say
  • Can John Lee learn from Carrie Lam’s mistakes and rebuild public trust?
  • Over 900 Chinese antiques, art pieces to be displayed in Hong Kong Palace Museum, including national treasures spanning 5,000 years
  • Hong Kong advocates call for better protection of pre-war building once used for spying against Japanese during World War II
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong logs 515 cases, clusters tied to bars in Central grow as authorities warn of rising infections
  • Amnesty International slams Hong Kong police for arresting 6 people over efforts to mark Tiananmen crackdown
  • June date set for China-sponsored Horn of Africa peace conference
  • Chinese debt traps in Africa? The bigger worry is bondholders, study finds
  • China services activity contracts for third straight month in May as coronavirus ‘still weighs heavy’
  • Tech war: Chinese database software vendor shrugs off sanctions risk on using open-source code from Oracle’s MySQL system
  • Coronavirus: how the Shanghai outbreak exposed the Chinese health system’s fragility
  • Joe Biden considers lifting some China tariffs to fight inflation
  • US plan to build ‘China house’ at State Department urgently needed but raises questions, analysts say
  • Shanghai reopening: 3 new community infections found as city officials warn residents of risk of Omicron resurgence
  • China’s corruption crackdown nets 8 finance sector official, Prada handbags – so why is it being ridiculed online?
  • Taiwan reports 124 Covid-19 deaths, including 9-year-old boy, but island ‘closer to peak’
  • Beijing to ease Covid-19 restaurant, school bans as local infections wane
  • Afghanistan appeals to China to help put out raging Nuristan forest fire
  • Coronavirus: Singapore should prepare for a new Covid-19 wave in July or August, health minister says
  • Malaysia palm group warns of losses ahead from ‘severe’ labour crunch
  • ‘Nothing less than hate speech’: Google ordered to pay Australian politician US$515,000 over defamatory YouTube videos
  • Jack Ma’s Ant Group unveils Singapore digital bank – ANEXT – in overseas expansion push
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese reaches out to Beijing over fighter jet encounter
  • How rising India’s Quad role is helping it find its place among global powers
  • US, South Korea launch eight missiles in response to North Korea missile firings
  • Accident or negligence? US naval officer in Japan faces prison over deadly crash – family claims mountain sickness
  • Philippine volcano spews ash and steam, in ‘abnormal conditions’
  • Cambodians face intimidation, threats as they head to polls for local election
  • How an Afghan woman who fled the Taliban is fighting for others like her
  • Massive blaze at Bangladesh container depot kills at least 49, injures more than 300
  • Some people still haven’t caught Covid-19. Why?
  • US must course-correct on gun violence after Texas school massacre to save itself
  • UK to give Ukraine long-range M270 rocket systems after Putin warnings
  • Russian general reportedly killed in eastern Ukraine, another high-ranking casualty
  • Russian foreign minister Lavrov’s trip to Serbia cancelled as neighbours close airspace
  • Ukraine’s President Zelensky visits close to war front line near Sievierodonetsk
  • Joe Biden scrambles to avoid Americas Summit flop as boycott threats cloud US plans
  • Embattled UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson set to face confidence vote
  • Can Joe Biden convince the US’ Middle East allies he still has their back?
  • Ukraine claims its forces control ‘half’ of Sievierodonetsk against Russia
  • UK government accused of trying to deport unaccompanied minors to Rwanda
  • Gulf states condemn Indian official’s ‘Islamophobic’ remarks about the Prophet Mohammed
  • Ethiopia accuses aid agencies of delivering banned equipment to Tigray that could be used by rebels
  • ‘Evil’: At least 50 killed in massacre at Catholic Church in southwest Nigeria
  • UK’s Queen Elizabeth ends Platinum Jubilee with surprise appearance
  • Nine dead, dozens wounded in weekend US mass shootings
  • New breast cancer drug could help thousands, researchers say
  • Pop superstar Ed Sheeran to crown Queen Elizabeth’s four-day jubilee jamboree
  • Russia hits Kyiv with missiles; Putin warns West about supplies
  • As Ukraine loses more and more troops, how long can it keep up the fight?
  • Fiscal spending a better tool than monetary easing to boost China’s economic growth
  • BYD, Meituan lead Hong Kong stock gains as Beijing rolls back Covid-19 curb while HKEX wobbles on LME lawsuit
  • Ant Group launches digital banking service in Singapore to serve small firms doing international trades
  • Hong Kong should stick to four-decade-old US dollar currency peg as it braces for ‘unusual challenges’ ahead, HKMA former CEO Yam says
  • Europe’s top money manager Amundi lends weight to Chinese stocks as market looks for sustained rebound
  • Like all wars, the US tech war on China is proving costly and pointless
  • Why we’ve not seen the end of stock market corrections or inflation
  • Climate change: How Chinese shipbuilding giant CSSC’s floating wind turbine will help advance the country’s decarbonisation drive
  • Developing tomorrow’s tech talents can fuel Hong Kong’s ambitions to become the next Silicon Valley
  • China gaming ban: no new licenses in May points to ‘new normal’ in regulatory hostility
  • Top shopping influencer Li Jiaqi a no-show at scheduled live stream after abrupt end to session on Friday
  • 2 unique places to visit in Malaysia but which of Penang’s Blue and Green Mansions is best? We compare tours, price and overall experience
  • Ancient Chinese porn served as sex education and was even used for fire prevention
  • Louis Vuitton, Rolex and Tag Heuer at 2022 Monaco Grand Prix highlight how luxury brands and sports have never been closer
  • Luxurious Urban Living in a Tranquil Hong Kong Haven
  • In City of Orange, YA writer David Yoon’s adult novel, unexplained desolation gives way to incapacitating heartbreak
  • K-drama Eve: Seo Ye-ji’s comeback, two years after It’s Okay to Not Be Okay, is standard high-society fare
  • What is microdosing? Using psychedelic drugs in tiny amounts regularly enhances your mood and creativity, its proponents argue. What does the science say?
  • What to wear to a wedding in 2022 – fashion experts’ tips for women and men on the best attire
  • The 13 best Japanese restaurants in Hong Kong: Sushi Wadatsumi, Shikigiku Japanese Restaurant, Sushi Saito at Four Seasons Hong Kong, Ryota Kappou Modern and Zuicho are all leading the wave
  • How the high jewellery market boomed in Asia during Covid-19: Chanel, Cartier, Bulgari, Van Cleef & Arpels and Graff used Zoom and private viewings for clients to maintain relationships
  • 7 Indian LGBT icons you need to know: from YouTuber Lilly Singh, Olympian Dutee Chand and gay royal Prince Manvendra Singh Gohil, to transgender activist Laxmi Narayan Tripathi and film director Onir
  • Inside Khloé Kardashian’s multi-million dollar car collection: from Porsche and Ferrari supercars to Lamborghini and Rolls-Royce SUVs to rival Kim – but does Kylie Jenner outshine them both?
  • Who’s the world’s richest DJ in 2022? Net worths, ranked: from Calvin Harris’ hits for Rihanna and Dua Lipa, and Steve Aoki’s NFTs, to Daft Punk’s retirement and David Guetta’s 50 million album sales
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Work to create Osaka Expo site threatens wild birds on island
  • Tohoku’s only bullfighting event celebrates 40th season
  • COVID-19 cases in Tokyo fall for 23rd day, but 2 related deaths

  • Footprints of monsters, energy beam celebrate ‘Shin Ultraman’
  • VOX POPULI: As weeds flourish in rainy season, a reflection on what they cover
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
